tooltips.cc: Cyclomatic Complexity: measures how many execution paths the code has. Higher numbers mean harder to test and maintain.
tooltips.riskLow: Low risk: simple code, easy to maintain (CC ≤ 5)
tooltips.riskModerate: Moderate risk: acceptable complexity, review carefully (CC 6-10)
tooltips.riskHigh: High risk: complex code, difficult to test (CC 11-20)
tooltips.riskCritical: Critical risk: very complex code, consider refactoring (CC > 20)
tooltips.confidence: Confidence: how certain the analysis is that this dependency exists. 100% = confirmed, lower = detected by heuristics.
tooltips.crud: CRUD: Create, Read, Update, Delete — the 4 basic data operations
tooltips.etl: ETL: Extract, Transform, Load — data flow pattern
tooltips.upstream: Upstream: procedures that call this one (who calls me)
tooltips.downstream: Downstream: procedures and tables that this one calls (who do I call)
tooltips.lineage: Lineage: traces the origin and destination of data through procedures
tooltips.exec: EXEC: SQL instruction that runs another stored procedure
tooltips.dynamic: Dynamic: the procedure name is built at runtime, making static analysis harder
tooltips.impact: Impact Analysis: shows what would be affected if this object is modified
tooltips.depth: Nesting depth: how many levels of IF/WHILE/TRY blocks are nested
tooltips.branches: Branches: number of conditional forks (IF, CASE, etc.)
